title: PDF attachments are corrupted after upload
body: When customers upload PDF files to their tasks, the files come through broken. You can't open them — the PDF viewer says the file is damaged. This is happening for multiple customers. We use the file attachment feature heavily so this is really impacting our workflow.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — corrupted file uploads are definitely something we want to get to the bottom of quickly.

Could you walk me through the exact steps you're taking to upload a PDF? Specifically:
- How are you uploading the file? (e.g., drag-and-drop into a task, the "Attach file" button, the API)
- After uploading, how are you downloading/opening it? (e.g., clicking the attachment link in the task, downloading via the API)
- Does this happen with all PDFs, or only certain ones? (e.g., large files, scanned documents, files from a specific source)

This will help us reproduce the issue on our end.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: We use the Attach file button in the task view. Then customers download by clicking the attachment link. Look, I don't know all the technical details — I just know PDFs are coming through broken and my team can't do their jobs. I've noticed that some smaller PDFs seem to work fine but the bigger ones are definitely messed up. Can you just fix this?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
I completely understand the urgency — we want to get this fixed for your team as quickly as possible.

One thing that would help us reproduce the issue on our end: could you let us know what browser your team is using (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Safari) and roughly how large the PDFs are that come through broken? For example, are we talking 5 MB, 20 MB, 100 MB+?

If you happen to know which version of TaskFlow you're on (you can usually find this under Settings → About), that would be helpful too, but the browser and file size are the most important pieces right now.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Chrome mostly, I think some of my team uses Firefox too. The broken ones are probably a few MB — like reports and contracts, stuff like that. I don't know the exact sizes. We're on whatever version was updated recently, maybe 2.3 something? I don't remember. This started about a week ago if that helps.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"PDF attachments corrupted on download — size-dependent, regression in ~2.3.x","problem":"PDF files uploaded via the 'Attach file' button in task view are corrupted when downloaded by clicking the attachment link. The PDF viewer reports the file as damaged. Smaller PDFs appear unaffected; larger PDFs (multi-page reports, contracts) are consistently broken. The issue affects multiple customers and began approximately one week ago.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent change in the ~2.3.x release likely introduced a bug in the file upload or download pipeline that corrupts larger files. Probable causes include: (1) a multipart/chunked upload reassembly bug truncating or misordering chunks, (2) an incorrect Content-Length or Transfer-Encoding header on download causing truncation, (3) a new file-size limit or middleware (e.g., compression, virus scanning) silently corrupting files above a threshold, or (4) a binary-vs-text encoding issue (e.g., base64 or UTF-8 transcoding applied to binary data) that only manifests when the file exceeds a buffer size.","reproduction_steps":["Open a task in TaskFlow 2.3.x","Click the 'Attach file' button","Upload a PDF larger than ~1-2 MB (e.g., a multi-page report or contract)","After upload completes, click the attachment link to download the file","Attempt to open the downloaded PDF — it should report as damaged/corrupted","Repeat with a very small PDF (<100 KB) to confirm it downloads correctly"],"environment":"TaskFlow ~2.3.x (recent update, approximately one week old), Chrome and Firefox, multiple customers affected","severity":"high","impact":"Multiple customers who rely on file attachments cannot share or retrieve PDF documents, blocking daily workflows for entire teams.","recommended_fix":"1. Check recent changes (past ~1-2 weeks) to the file upload/download pipeline, storage layer, or any middleware (compression, antivirus, CDN config). 2. Compare the byte content of an uploaded large PDF in storage against the original — identify where corruption occurs (upload vs. storage vs. download). 3. Check for chunked transfer or multipart upload changes. 4. Verify Content-Type and Content-Length headers on download responses. 5. Check for any new file-size limits or transcoding logic.","proposed_test_case":"Upload PDFs of varying sizes (100 KB, 1 MB, 5 MB, 20 MB) via the 'Attach file' button, download each via the attachment link, and verify the downloaded file's SHA-256 checksum matches the original. This should be an automated integration test that covers the full upload-store-download round trip.","information_gaps":["Exact file sizes that trigger corruption (threshold is unclear beyond 'a few MB')","Exact TaskFlow version (reporter said '2.3 something')","Whether non-PDF file types are also affected","Server-side error logs from the upload/download endpoints"]}
